visualGoal: Make a simmer buy pedals at the RIGHT time and by the RIGHT mechanism (sliding vs low-profile vs pendular vs force-feedback), not day-one and not by brand prestige. Real pedal product proof throughout; no procedural art. Only the Honeycomb Charlie has a verified photo, so real photos of the rest of the roster are the top visual need.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: Budget all-rounder and the article's recommended first buy. Image file exists on disk but is NOT in verified-product-images.json, so it is currently a labeled Honeycomb Charlie stand-in. Verify or re-source before it can appear as its own gearpick/shotgrid or before certification.

No-toe-brake combat/warbird pick. Existing on-disk file is the Mk.IV image and is NOT verified; the gear page id is still vkb-t-rudder-mk4. Source a verified Mk.V photo (and consider renaming the id to mk5) before real visual or certification.
The only serious Xbox+PC pedal option. Image file exists on disk but is NOT verified; currently a labeled Charlie stand-in.
Premium pendular flagship, text-and-link only in body. No catalog entry and no image yet. Add flight.json entry + gear page + verified photo to make it a body visual.
Cheapest entry, text-and-link only. No catalog entry and no image yet.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic home flight-sim desk at golden-hour rim light, a real pair of rudder pedals under the desk on a rubber anti-slip mat, a yoke and throttle above on the desk, empty floor space beside the pedals left open for a real product overlay, curator Mac Donovan seated with feet on the pedals mid-crosswind-correction, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, fake pedals, warped controls, impossible ergonomics, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art

infographicPrompts.title: The four rudder-pedal mechanisms
content: Pendular (swings on a pivot, premium feel)

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult flight-sim curator Mac Donovan in a clean home cockpit bay, feet on a pair of rudder pedals, one hand gesturing to a mat under the pedals, calm instructor energy, product overlay space beside him, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.
